variants.description: Keeps all outputs at the low rail and therefore has no DCO, feedback, lock, or control behavior.
Generates the clocks but drives lock high continuously, missing disturbance and reacquire behavior.
Uses the wrong feedback divider ratio so late-window feedback cadence cannot track the reference.
Keeps the control voltage fixed instead of correcting after the reference frequency step.
Reports a control-voltage monitor outside the supply rails while otherwise preserving most clock behavior.
